MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Janine M. Fiel-Cosse of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party William Andrews.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date of December 9, 2019 is advanced to August 26, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is September 5, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 22, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 9, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 23, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 7, 2019. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
